title: Present Simple – Positive
description: How to form positive sentences in the Present Simple

lessons.title: Positive Structure
lessons.content:
**Structure:** Subject + Verb

For most pronouns, we do NOT change the verb.
For **he / she / it** (third-person singular), add **'s'** to the verb.

| Pronoun | Verb | Example |
|---------|------|---------||
| I | eat | I eat apples. |
| You | eat | You eat apples. |
| We | eat | We eat apples. |
| They | eat | They eat apples. |
| He | eat**s** | He eats apples. |
| She | eat**s** | She eats apples. |
| It | eat**s** | It eats apples. |

lessons.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
lessons.content:
⚠️ Do NOT use 'am / is / are' before the verb.
⚠️ Do NOT end the verb with '-ing'.

| ❌ Incorrect | ✅ Correct |
|-------------|----------|
| She **is** study French. | She **studies** French. |
| I **am** understand you. | I **understand** you. |
| We **are** living in Haifa. | We **live** in Haifa. |
| He **working** out every day. | He **works** out every day. |

lessons.title: Spelling Rules for Third-Person Singular
lessons.content:
When adding 's' for he/she/it:

🔤 **Most verbs:** just add **-s** → like → likes, want → wants
🔤 **Verbs ending in -ch, -sh, -x, -s, -z:** add **-es** → watch → watches, fix → fixes
🔤 **Verbs ending in consonant + y:** change **y → ies** → study → studies, fly → flies
🔤 **Irregular:** go → goes, do → does, have → has

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form of the verb.
exercises.sentence: I usually {_} to school in the morning.
exercises.explanation: First person singular (I) — no change to the verb.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form of the verb.
exercises.sentence: We {_} to music in the car.
exercises.explanation: First person plural (We) — no change to the verb.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form of the verb.
exercises.sentence: We {_} along with the songs on the radio.
exercises.explanation: First person plural (We) — no change to the verb.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form of the verb.
exercises.explanation: Third person singular (John/He) — add 's' to the verb.

exercises.explanation: Third person plural (My parents/They) — no change to the verb.

exercises.explanation: Third person singular (Sarah/She) — add 's' to the verb.

exercises.explanation: Second person (You) — no change to the verb.

exercises.explanation: First person singular (I) — no change to the verb.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form of the verb.
exercises.explanation: Third person singular (The dog/It) — add 's' to the verb.

exercises.explanation: Third person singular (Jim/He) — add 's' to the verb.

exercises.explanation: First person plural (We) — no change to the verb.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form of the verb.
exercises.sentence: Once a year, she {_} to Switzerland.
exercises.explanation: Third person singular (She) — 'fly' ends in consonant+y, so change y→ies: flies.

exercises.explanation: Third person singular (He) — add 's' to the verb.

exercises.explanation: Third person singular (The conference/It) — add 's' to the verb.

exercises.explanation: Third person singular (Ice/It) — add 's' to the verb.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form of the verb.
exercises.sentence: I normally {_} singing in the afternoon.
exercises.explanation: First person singular (I) — no change to the verb.

exercises.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form of the verb.
exercises.sentence: I {_} that is a good idea.
exercises.explanation: 'Think' is a stative verb. First person singular (I) — no change.
Do not use '-ing' in the Present Simple. Remove 'ing': We speak English.
Third person singular (She) — add 's': She lives in Jerusalem.
Do not use 'am/is/are' before the verb. 'Love' is a stative verb: I love my parents.
Do not use '-ing' in the Present Simple: We study together every week.

exercises.sentence: The dog is want the bone.
exercises.explanation: Do not use 'is' before the verb. Third person singular needs 's': The dog wants the bone.
Do not use 'are' before the verb in the Present Simple: You work hard.
'Know' is a stative verb. Do not use 'are' before it: They know Hungarian.

exercises.sentence: I once a week eating with my family.
answers: I eat with my family once a week.
exercises.explanation: Do not use '-ing'. Time phrases go at the beginning or end: I eat with my family once a week.
Third person singular (Andy/He) — add 's': Andy makes good food.

exercises.sentence: I reading every morning the newspaper.
answers: I read the newspaper every morning.
exercises.explanation: Do not use '-ing'. Time phrases go at the end: I read the newspaper every morning.

exercises.sentence: I usually {_} at 8 in the morning.
exercises.explanation: First person singular (I) — use the base form 'wake up'. No auxiliary verb.
'Love' is a stative verb. Third person plural (They) — use the base form 'love'.
Third person plural (My parents/They) — use the base form 'work'. No 's' added.
Second person (You) — use the base form 'go'. No auxiliary verb.
Third person singular (She) — add 's': travels.
Third person singular (He) — add 's': comes.
First person singular (I) — use the base form 'shower'. No 's'.
First person plural (We) — use the base form 'sleep'. No auxiliary.
Third person plural (Children/They) — use the base form 'play'.

exercises.sentence: My mom {_} me at least once a week.
exercises.explanation: Third person singular (My mom/She) — add 's': calls.

exercises.sentence: Once a month we {_} our family.
exercises.explanation: First person plural (We) — use the base form 'visit'.
